dublinCore.dc:title: 119 HR 8048 IH: American Indian and Alaska Native Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

action.action-desc: Mrs. Grijalva introduced the following bill; which was referred to the Committee on Education and Workforce

form.official-title: To amend the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act to require that equitable distribution of assistance include equitable distribution to Indian Tribes and Tribal organizations, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the American Indian and Alaska Native Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act, the AI/AN Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act, or AI/AN CAPTA.

section.header: Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act amendments

subsection.text: Section 108(b) of the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act (42 U.S.C. 5106d(b)) is amended, in the first sentence, by inserting Indian Tribes, and Tribal organizations, after the States,.
Section 203 of the Child Abuse Prevention and Treatment Act (42 U.S.C. 5116b) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking section 210 each place it appears and inserting section 209; and
in subsection (a), by striking 1 percent and all that follows through the period at the end and inserting the following: “, of the amount appropriated under section 209 for a fiscal year—
5 percent of the amount to make allotments to Indian Tribes and Tribal organizations; and
1 percent of the amount to make allotments to migrant programs.
